summary_text: Verification lane findings (focused on attribution, plagiarism, claim verification): - `High` Copyright/licensing risk: reusing the embedded image outside X’s native quote/repost flow can be treated as unauthorized re-publication. - `High` Plagiarism risk: copying core wording, hook, or structure too closely from the referenced post without explicit attribution. - `High` Claim-verification risk: repeating numbers, benchmarks, or factual claims from the third-party post without checking primary sources/date context. - `Medium` False endorsement risk: phrasing that implies the original author endorses your post or collaborated. - `Medium` Context distortion risk: summarizing a single post in a way that changes intent or omits key caveats. - `Medium` Privacy/rights risk: image may contain faces/logos/content with additional rights constraints. Operational guardrails: 1. Prefer `Quote Post` if you want to use the same image/content context safely on X. 2. If posting separately, avoid reuploading the image unless you have permission; use your own image instead. 3. Add clear attribution (`Inspired by @...` + original URL). 4. Rewrite with original analysis/value-add; do not mirror phrasing or structure. 5. Verify each factual claim against a primary source (or mark as unverified and soften wording). 6. Avoid wording that implies endorsement by the original author.
